items.title: 'I don't want a circus': ACT Education Minister Yvette Berry resigns from cabinet

children.content: ACT Education Minister Yvette Berry resigns after the Integrity Commission made "serious corrupt conduct" findings against two senior public servants in her portfolio.

items.title: Serious corruption findings against senior ACT public servants

children.content: The commission investigated whether officials were impartial and honest in awarding an $18.8 million refurbishment contract at Campbell Primary School to Lendlease, despite a bid by local firm Manteena being deemed better and cheaper.

image.altText: Public servants and school sign

items.title: 'Bleak motorways' in Canberra's south to gain housing under draft plan

children.content: The plan is for mid-rise housing to be developed closest to transport stops and shopping centres, with high-rise development reserved for the Woden town centre and north Curtin.

image.altText: Southern transport corridor aerial

items.title: 'I was in tears': Living without a heater through Canberra's winter

children.content: Barbara Belmonte, an ACT public housing resident, has been waiting weeks for her heater to be repaired amid the sub-zero temperatures of a Canberra winter — and she is not the only one.

image.altText: Barbara Belmont, public housing resident, sits and gestures while talking.

items.title: Home Affairs asks DV survivors to prove abuser relationship 'genuine'

children.content: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

image.altText: An anonymous woman in the city, back of head

items.title: 'Her life is ruined': Hellenic Club cleared of failing to record problem gambling

children.content: The Hellenic Club has been cleared of breaches of failing to record the problem gambling of a Canberra couple despite the pair losing at least $345,000 on poker machines.

items.title: Unemployment rate steady for now, but could increase in coming months

children.content: The unemployment rate was steady in June at 4.4 per cent but underemployment has risen.

image.altText: Shoppers on Pitt Street mall on Boxing Day

items.title: OpenAI model goes rogue during testing and hacks startup

children.content: OpenAI has announced one of its AI models broke containment during testing before hacking into another AI startup.

image.altText: Sam Altman standing in front of the OpenAI logo.

items.title: Researcher shocked as orca blows up a sunfish in front of her

children.content: Underwater video captures the moment killer whales in the Gulf of California slam into a sunfish, causing it to explode.
Researchers say the orcas may use the technique to make it easier for juvenile killer whales to feed or they do it as a fun group bonding exercise.

gallery.altText: Two black and white whale sharks in a cloud of white fleshy scraps against a blue backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: Two black and white whale sharks in a cloud of white fleshy scraps against a blue backdrop.

avatar.alt: A man in a blue checked shirt and blue jacket with a beard and long hair.

items.title: Judge rejects Woodside's attempt to uncover stink-bomb protest organisers

children.content: Energy giant Woodside fails in its Supreme Court bid to force climate activists to hand over documents about the planning of a stink-bomb protest which prompted the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

image.altText: woodside

items.title: Origin Energy shares drop back slightly after company admits data hack

children.content: Shares in Origin Energy have given up ground after the company confirmed a customer data hack. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Job seeker

items.title: Mamdani says he cannot arrest Netanyahu in New York but US government can

children.content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani concedes he cannot order the arrest of Benjamin Netanyahu and calls on the US government to take action, but a former international prosecutor warns it could be complicated.

articles.title: US and Saudi Arabia reach nuclear power deal

children.content: Israeli officials and US politicians from both parties have voiced concern that a civilian nuclear project in Saudi Arabia could be used to eventually develop nuclear weapons.

image.altText: Donald Trump holding hands with Mohammed Bin Salman during a meeting in the Oval Office.

articles.title: Trump threatens Iranian power plants, bridges if ships attacked in Hormuz

children.content: US strikes have already widened from the south to western and central areas of Iran in recent days.

image.altText: A woman in Islamic dress walks on a street under a billboard showing a MAGA hat and the words "Hey terrorist. Get ready to die"

gallery.altText: Mamdani sits with his hands clasped at a wooden desk.

thumbnail.altText: Mamdani sits with his hands clasped at a wooden desk.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Right-wing conference arrives in UK to snoring, empty seats and bad reviews

children.content: In 10 years at the ABC, I've had some strange experiences. I can now add last week's CPAC conference in London to the list, and it's going somewhere near the top, says Riley Stuart in London.

image.altText: A wide shot of a half full auditorium with a woman in blue dress on stage in distance

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: The 'fairy gold dust passport' more Australians are chasing

children.content: Australians are racing to secure Irish citizenship, with new figures showing applications have more than doubled in the past three years as people seek the right to live, work and study across the EU.

image.altText: A family of four taking a selfie infront of the colloseum in Rome

items.title: Reading has many benefits, here’s how to fit it in to a busy life

children.content: More than half of us want to read more. Here's how to find your gateway genre and make that happen.

gallery.altText: Overhead shot of woman holding a book and a phone with an Instagram feed in either hand.

thumbnail.altText: Overhead shot of woman holding a book and a phone with an Instagram feed in either hand.

items.title: Councils mark six years until Brisbane 2032 Olympic Games

children.content: The Brisbane City Council has installed a countdown clock to mark the milestone, while an iconic Kangaroo mascot has returned in Moreton Bay.

image.altText: A woman and a man pose with their hands up in front of a digital clock.

items.title: The intriguing film that casts the 'superior' Jude Law as the insidious Putin

children.content: Cannes darling Oliver Assayas tackles the shadowy forces of our time in his latest film, The Wizard of the Kremlin.

image.altText: A man in a suit talks on the phone

items.title: Record $60k fine for man behind 'brazen' dump site that will cost $1m to clean

children.content: The owner of one of Victoria's worst illegal dump sites has been handed a record fine of $60,000 and ordered to pay an expected $1 million to clean up the polluted property in Melbourne's outer north-east.

gallery.altText: A large pile of rubbish at a dump with smoke on a regional property

thumbnail.altText: A large pile of rubbish at a dump with smoke on a regional property

items.title: Nintendo raised prices due to US tariffs. Now, it doesn't want to pass on refunds

children.content: Nintendo has argued it has no duty to pass its refunds onto US consumers. It's the latest in a lengthy tariff fight.

image.altText: A handheld gaming device.

items.title: Tongan women 'auctioned off' for marriage in China

children.content: The ABC has spoken to multiple Tongan women who have been lured into an arranged marriage syndicate operating in China, with allegations of passports being confiscated and tens of thousands of dollars changing hands.

image.altText: Close-up of a woman wearing a face mask.

items.title: From Mumbai to New York, young Indians gather in unprecedented movement

children.content: A student who started a youth uprising across India is fast becoming the biggest challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's 12-year reign.

gallery.altText: Young protesters on a city street, some holding India flags.

thumbnail.altText: Young protesters on a city street, some holding India flags.

items.title: 'Nervous' investors retreat from housing market as downturn broadens

children.content: Australia's property market has entered a downturn after both house and unit prices fell for the first time in more than three years, bringing an end to the recent housing boom.

image.altText: a skyline with houses

items.title: Japanese PM's sleep revelation sparks concern

children.content: It's unlikely Sanae Takaichi intended to start a national conversation when she tweeted about how much rest she gets. A sleep expert says her schedule would be exacting a heavy toll.

image.altText: Woman in light blue blazer

items.title: Sydney man's home gets so hot he has to replace his TV

children.content: A new study led by Western Sydney University researchers has found a lack of thermal safety standards in social housing is leading to tenants being exposed to unsafe extreme heat events.

gallery.altText: a man wearing a blue t-shirt standing at his front door

thumbnail.altText: a man wearing a blue t-shirt standing at his front door

items.title: Rebel Wilson's victory in defamation case could be short-lived. Here's why

children.content: As lawyers for actor Charlotte MacInnes plan to appeal a failed defamation case outcome against Hollywood star Rebel Wilson, a solicitor says the judge's interpretations could be grounds for a retrial or a different verdict.

image.altText: Blonde woman in pink and white top smiles

items.title: How One Nation is winning over the bush

children.content: One Nation has some of its strongest support in outback Australia and recent polling shows it could win over 30 regional seats. So what's winning over regional Australians when it comes to Pauline Hanson's party?

image.altText: A red-haired woman is pictured on a billboard of a pool with the message $13 million Cooee Bay Pool delivered

items.title: Hobart council snack-saga motion to be heard behind closed doors

children.content: Calls for an independent investigation into Hobart's Deputy Lord Mayor will be debated behind closed doors, as a stoush over town hall snacks continues.

image.altText: A woman and a person whose identity has been obscured outside a doorway.

items.title: Prince George turns 13 as he heads to Eton

children.content: Prince George, second in line to the throne, has turned 13 as he readies to follow in his father Prince William's footsteps and start at the prestigious Eton College.

image.altText: A boy in a black suit and white shirt, smiling

items.title: How we got to Glasgow 2026 and what the Commonwealth Games will look like

children.content: The bumpy road to Glasgow 2026 has produced a Commonwealth Games that will look a little different than the ones that came before.

articles.title: Nina Kennedy knows now is the time to soak it all in — if she remembers to

children.content: Australia's Commonwealth Games flag-bearer is trying to stop and smell the roses after a brutal year that threatened to expedite the end of her career.

image.altText: Nina Kennedy with the Australian flag after being announced as Australia's flag-bearer at the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

articles.title: Sharks 'happy to take underdog tag' ahead of blockbuster World Cup

children.content: The Aussie Sharks are more than content to take ownership of the underdog tag when they host Spain in their opening match of the World Aquatics World Cup event on Thursday. But underestimate them at your peril.

image.altText: Australian players celebrate with their arms in the air

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Commonwealth Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

gallery.altText: Swimmer Duncan Scott with 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games mascots Clyde and Finnie.

thumbnail.altText: Swimmer Duncan Scott with 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games mascots Clyde and Finnie.

items.title: Aussie captain relishes 'special' chance 'to take time to be a dad'

children.content: As Nathan Power looks to lead the Australian Sharks at the World Aquatics Water Polo World Cup Finals in Sydney this week, the 33-year-old reflects on how fatherhood has changed his outlook, and how valuable his six-month paternity leave is.

image.altText: Nathan Power with his partner Maddy and son Sebastian standing in a trophy

items.title: Why netball is growing into a sport of choice for men and boys

children.content: More young men are taking up netball at a competitive level. Competition organisers say there is less stigma around the sport among boys, with plenty of talent emerging.

image.altText: A man in a white top holding a netball with a medal around his neck

items.title: Rusted tin transformed into prize-winning portraits

children.content: Pilbara artist Jenna Pickering transforms found bush objects into hyper-realistic portraits.

gallery.altText: Detailed painting of a man on a piece of tin.
A woman holds a piece of metal above her head and makes a funny face in front of an old building.
